messages.content:
It's still not working. I changed the getWString signature to

const LPWSTR getWString(std::string str, const wchar_t* def, wchar_t*& buffer)

and I changed the type of ret to const LPWSTR as you suggested, but there continues to be an error in the line assigning to ret. The error says “a value of type ‘const w_char_t *’ cannot be used to initialize an entity of type 'const LPWSTR'”

Also, there is another problem. Near the end of the function is this line:
ret = (LPWSTR)buffer;

but if ret is made const, this line produces an error since ret is not modifiable.

How should I fix these problems?

That still produced an error on the LPWSTR ret = def; line, but I have solved the error by using the LPCWSTR type instead of the LPWSTR type. (I have not yet confirmed that the compiler warning is fixed)

However, now I have a new problem. In another function is this line:

mii.dwTypeData = getWString(msg, L"Reset window size", buffer);

It has the error  “a value of type ‘LPCWSTR’ cannot be assigned to an entity of type ‘LPWSTR’”

mii is of type MENUITEMINFOW, a struct, and dwTypeData is of type LPWSTR. They are defined in the Windows library file WinUser.h, so I can't modify MENUITEMINFOW.

Any suggestions on resolving this error?

Those changes cause some errors in the str_replace function, where the variable ins is assigned to. ins is of type char*.

The line:
ins = orig;
has an error because a value of type "const char *" cannot be assigned to an entiry of type "char *".

The line:
ins = strstr(orig, rep);
also has the same error.
